description: Stage 3.4 v3: Eval-time optimization and late branching on Era 6 stack. One implemented patch (late_branch_finishers). SLOT, n-gram bias, and ensemble eval are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — they need patches written. The branching machinery is the consequential code; the eval-time ideas are the consequential hypotheses.

benchmarks.frontier_pr: PR #1487 (ndokutovich) SP8192 + Recur345 + Par7 + PreQuant TTT 10ep
benchmarks.slot_pr: PR #1507 (L-BFGS SLOT with entropy-adaptive n-gram mixer)

lane_bc_bakeoff.description: Lane B/C: load external checkpoint (CHECKPOINT_PATH env), apply export/eval patches. No training.

decision.description: Full-box eval for promoted winners. Composites allowed.

slots.name: baseline_export_control
slots.why: Era 6 checkpoint + TTT 5ep + standard GPTQ + sliding window eval. All candidates must beat this.
slots.validates: The Era 6 export pipeline produces expected deployed score.
slots.falsifies: All comparisons invalid if control diverges.
notes: Includes TTT and sliding window eval as defaults.

slots.name: raw_ema_deploy_triple
slots.mechanism: Branch at 75% wallclock into 3 export-state finishers: raw (no EMA), EMA-heavy (decay=0.9995), deploy-QAT (moderate EMA + surrogate). Export best post-GPTQ branch.
slots.math: At t=0.75*T, fork into 3. Each runs for (T-t)/3. Export argmin(post_quant_bpb).
slots.placement: In training loop at wallclock check. Patch exists in patches.py:22.
slots.shapes: 3 copies of model state. No new parameters per branch.
slots.coefficients: branch_start_frac=0.75. Raw: use_ema=0. EMA-heavy: decay=0.9995. Deploy: decay=0.999, surrogate=0.0005.
slots.failure_modes: Each branch gets too little time (~8% of total). Or TTT washes out branch differences.
slots.why: Export-state choice matters enormously on the frontier. This tests raw vs EMA vs deploy-aligned by running all three.
slots.kill_rule: No branch separates from control, or winning branch is always default EMA.
notes: Patch EXISTS in patches.py:22. Patch applies cleanly to base_train_gpt.py.

slots.name: aggressive_tri_branch
slots.mechanism: Same branching infrastructure as H1 but harder-swinging finishers: QAT alpha=0.7, frozen embeds, boosted matrix LR.
slots.math: Branch at 0.72*T. Deploy-QAT-aggr: alpha=0.7, surrogate=0.0009. Family-split: lr_matrix*=1.2, lr_embed=0.
slots.placement: Same patch as H1, different env config. Patch exists in patches.py:22.
slots.failure_modes: Aggressive finishers destabilize. Or branching 4% earlier weakens trunk.
slots.why: Branching gives permission to swing harder. If H1 shows branching works, H2 tests max aggression.
slots.kill_rule: No branch beats control, or winner is always conservative EMA.
notes: Same patch as H1 — different BRANCH_SPEC_JSON. Patch applies cleanly.

slots.name: ngram_logit_bias
slots.mechanism: At eval time, compute n-gram frequency tables from prior context. Add weighted logit bias to model output. Zero training cost.
slots.math: logit_bias(v) = 0.15 * log(1 + count_bi(v)) + 0.10 * log(1 + count_tri(v)). logits_final = logits_model + logit_bias.
slots.placement: In eval loop, after model forward, before loss. Patch in patches.py:351.
slots.shapes: N-gram tables: hash maps. Logit bias: [V=8192] per position.
slots.failure_modes: Model already captures n-gram patterns. Or fixed alpha/beta wrong for some sequences.
slots.why: PR #1507 shows n-gram mixing is enormously powerful. Even simple bias is a free lunch at eval time.

slots.name: slot_per_sample_optimization
slots.mechanism: SLOT: per-sample L-BFGS optimization of a logit bias vector at eval time. Each sample gets 20 L-BFGS steps to find a bias that minimizes its own loss.
slots.math: delta_s = argmin_{delta} L(model(x; delta), x). delta: [V=8192]. L-BFGS max_iter=20, lr=0.1.
slots.placement: In eval loop, wrapping forward pass. Patch in patches.py:481.
slots.shapes: delta: [8192] per sample. L-BFGS state: ~3 * 8192 floats.
slots.failure_modes: Eval protocol disallows per-sample optimization. Or L-BFGS too slow.
slots.why: PR #1507 reaches 0.2282 BPB with SLOT. This is the paradigm shift — eval-time compute matters as much as training.
slots.kill_rule: Eval protocol disallows it, or L-BFGS doesn't converge.
notes: Highest expected value if eval protocol allows it.
Must verify competition rules before investing implementation effort.

slots.name: aggressive_ttt_10ep
slots.mechanism: Push TTT to 10 epochs, unfreeze block 1, add cosine LR decay within TTT. More params + more epochs + scheduled LR = deeper adaptation.
slots.math: TTT: 10 epochs, lr=0.0004 * cosine_decay, freeze=[0] only. Start from EMA(w_T).
slots.placement: Post-training TTT loop, after EMA apply, before export. Patch in patches.py:595.
slots.coefficients: epochs=10, lr=0.0004, freeze=[0], cosine within TTT.
slots.failure_modes: Overfits to val data. Exceeds wallclock. Unfreezing block 1 destabilizes.
slots.why: Default TTT (5ep) is conservative. PR #1487 gets -0.034 with 10ep. Our 0.103 gap demands maximum TTT exploitation.
notes: Directly attacks the largest known lever.
